DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2025 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS JAMES KILPATRICK, AND LAKESIDE INVESTMENT COMPANY LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (James Kilpatrick and Lakeside Investment Company, LLC) demurrer to the fifth cause of action for equitable indemnity is overruled.

The issue under section 430.10(e) is, taking the facts properly pleaded and noticed as true, does the challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a cause for relief. (CCP 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019), 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, the complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with a statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) The court liberally construes the complaint per CCP 452.

"The right to implied contractual indemnity is predicated upon the indemnitor's breach of contract, 'the rationale ... being that a contract under which the indemnitor undertook to do work or perform services necessarily implied an obligation to do the work involved in a proper manner and to discharge foreseeable damages resulting from improper performance absent any participation by the indemnitee in the wrongful act precluding recovery.' ... 'An action for implied contractual indemnity is not a claim for contribution from a joint tortfeasor; it is not founded upon a tort or upon any duty which the indemnitor owes to the injured third party. It is grounded upon the indemnitor's breach of duty owing to the indemnitee to properly perform its contractual duties.' " (West v. Superior Court (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 1625, 1633 (internal citations and italics omitted).)

Here, plaintiff sufficiently states a claim for implied contractual indemnity, which is a form of equitable indemnity. (Bay Development Ltd. v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 50 Cal.3d 1012, 1029.

Plaintiff alleges that it was required to pay the insurance premiums that defendants requested and benefitted defendants, defendants failed to use reasonable care in performing the insurance and brokerage agreements, and defendants' dereliction damaged plaintiff. (CACI 3801; First Amended Complaint "(FAC"), pars. 1, 11-20.) A fair reading of the complaint indicates that defendants failed to use reasonable care in effectuating the "Proposal for Coverage." (FAC, pars. 18-19, Ex. B.)

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported . (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS JAMES KILPATRICK, AND LAKESIDE INVESTMENT COMPANY LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (James Kilpatrick and Lakeside Investment Company, LLC) motion to strike the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is granted with 20 days leave to amend.

The FAC presently does not plead a contractual basis for the attorney's fees. (CCP 1021.) In addition, plaintiff's reliance on the tort of another doctrine to recover fees as damages is misplaced. Plaintiff does not allege that theory of recovery. "The doctrine allows a plaintiff to recover attorney fees if he is required to employ counsel to prosecute or defend an action against a third party because of the tort of defendant." (Behniwal v. Mix (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1027, 1043 (internal quotations omitted).) Here, plaintiff alleges that defendants (James Kilpatrick, Lakeside Investment Company, LLC, 1000 Van Ness LP, and 1000 Van Ness Owners Association) breached their joint obligation to pay the insurance premiums and commission. There is no "third-party" litigation. Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead a basis for attorney's fees if it has some other basis.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
